channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: How NYC congestion pricing tolls could impact drivers
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – New York Gov. Kathy Hochul recently introduced a new plan to toll drivers who enter Manhattan below 60th Street. The revised congestion pricing plan would cost most drivers $9 during peak hours and $2.25 during off-peak hours. It's expected to go into effect in January.
item.dc:creator: Dan Mannarino, PIX 11

item.title: Trump expected to name investor Scott Bessent as Treasury secretary pick
item.description: President-elect Trump is expected to tap hedge fund manager Scott Bessent to serve as Treasury secretary in his new administration, a source confirmed to NewsNation.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, NewsNation

item.title: Judge reduces prison sentence for Capitol rioter who berated and insulted him
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal judge on Friday imposed a one-year reduction in a prison sentence for a man who stormed the U.S. Capitol and then engaged in a pattern of disruptive courtroom behavior, including berating and insulting the judge.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL KUNZELMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump encounters Senate GOP limits with failed Gaetz AG push
item.description: President-elect Trump's ill-fated nomination of former Rep. Matt Gaetz (R-Fla.) to run the Justice Department marked an early sign of his potential limits in moving his nominees and dealing with Senate Republicans, giving him less wiggle room to get his Cabinet in place.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Florida officials allege 'pervasive' discrimination against Trump supporters at FEMA
item.description: Florida Attorney General Ashley Moody and U.S. Rep. Vern Buchanan will hold a news conference Friday "to discuss their efforts to uncover evidence of political discrimination at FEMA and demand accountability," according to a release from Buchanan's office.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, WFLA, NBC
